texts.full_text: данных: 12:30:45 18:45:56 Пример выходных данных (ответ на пример): 6 15 11 Чтобы решить эту задачу, нужно выполнить следующие шаги: 1. Разделите строки с временем по двоеточиям и преобразуйте полученные части в числа. 2. Преобразуйте каждое время из формата "часы:минуты:секунды" в количество секунд от начала дня (0 часов 0 минут 0 секунд). 3. Вычтите первое значение времени из второго, чтобы получить разницу во времени. 4. Преобразуйте полученное число секунд обратно к формату "часы:минуты:секунды". Пример кода на Python: ```python def time_difference(time1_str, time2_str): # Разделение строк и преобразование в числа h1, m1, s1 = map(int, time1_str.split(':')) h2, m2, s2 = map(int, time2_str.split(':')) # Преобразуем время из "часы:минуты:секунды" к секундам от начала дня total_seconds_1 = (h1 60) + s1 total_seconds_2 = (h2 60) + s2 # Вычисляем разницу во времени в секундах и преобразовываем обратно к "часы:минуты:секунды" difference_seconds = total_seconds_2 - total_seconds_1 hours, remainder = divmod(difference_seconds, 3600) minutes, seconds = divmod(remainder, 60) return f"{hours} {minutes} {seconds}" # пример использования функции: time_difference("12:30:45", "18:45:56") ``` Этот код вернет

texts.full_text: began as a rural area and transitioned to suburban residential development by 1950 when it was annexated into Philadelphia. It continues its evolution today with the construction industry developing new housing in some areas while others have been redeveloping old industrial spaces for modern use. The name "Wissinoming" comes from an English translation of a Native American term, which means 'the place where two rivers meet.' This is appropriate given that Wisinomering station was located near the intersections between both Amtrak's Northeast Corridor and SEPTA Regional Rail lines. The former rail line runs along what used to be known as Tacony Creek in old maps. Though it may seem small, this railway stop once served a vital role for Philadelphia residents during its operational years by providing an important link within the city. It is also worth noting that Wissinoming station was not only significant due to commuting but could have played part of historical significance as well. During World War II and Vietnam era, many soldiers would pass through this stop on their way to or from service. Even though it has been over 35 years since the last train departed, people still reminisce about Wissinoming station with fond memories. The old photos taken at that time bring back a nostalgic sense of Philadelphia's rich railway history. In some ways, Wisinomering stands as an iconic reminder to Philadelphians and visitors alike of how far this city has come since its inception. texts.full_text: Structural determinants of antibiotic allergy. Allergies to antibiotics, mainly the beta-lactam antibiotics (penicillins and cephalosporins), are a common, costly, and potentially dangerous clinical problem encountered in everyday practice. Although studies on the role of non-beta-lactam antibiotics in allergic diseases, particularly the development of special bacterial allergy to beta lactams have been limited by their rarity as compared with penicillin or cephalosporin sensitization and cross-sensitivity between other classes. With this limitation there is evidence that exposure through non-beta-lactam bacteria can play a role in the development of allergic responses after previous exposures from other alpha lactams. In contrast, studies on risk factors for developing antibiotic allergies have centered largely or solely on penicillin allergy and cephalosporin hypersensitivity. These include both biological as well environmental predisposing conditions such that patients with a family history are at higher odds of having an allergic response to medications compared those without. A number other findings indicate the possibility for non-genetic influences in antibiotic allergies, including similarities between people' immune system responses and exposure patterns during lifetime which can have varying impacts on susceptibility. However it has been identified there is evidence that early-life exposures can influence subsequent sensitization or desensitizations to medications. Therefore a complete understanding of the factors for allergy development will involve consideration not only biological but also environmental influences, as well their relationship with individual's immune system responses and exposure behaviors during lifetime.

During the planned refuelling, a television inspection of the irradiated fuel was carried out using specialised equipment, which showed that after five irradiation cycles, all RK-3 assemblies retained their original geometry, and all fuel elements were hermetically sealed. “No damage or changes in the elements of the working cassettes were found,” says the document signed by the Kola NPP. Of course, it is quite possible that Forteo can help more severely osteoporotic patients than MFP-Fluoride can, and there is nothing bad about patients and doctors having treatment options. It's just a shame that MFP-Fluoride will not be one of them. The scientific proof that MFP-Fluoride is a safe and effective treatment for osteoporosis Overview Update Safety-Control Dosage Special Relevant Publications Recent Scientific Publications Relevant Abstract Citations from The World Congress on Osteoporosis. MFP-Fluorides for Dental Caries The end of the story? Sadly, Marcus G. Grodberg passed away June 5, 2002, his decades long effort to make fluoride available for the treatment of osteoporosis still short of fruition. Despite scientifically valid and statistically significant findings that low-dose MFP-Fluoride was (at the time) unique among osteoporosis treatments in stimulating bone formation (as opposed to slowing down bone loss), the commercial "barriers to entry" set up by the FDA and the drug companies have kept this, like so many other effective and low-cost treatments, off the market. This site will remain available as a reference for the immediate future, in the hopes that it may be of some support to those still carrying on the battle and to those considering joining it. We also hope the information here will help warn those considering using other fluoride products as "off-label" treatments for osteoporosis about the dangers of doing so. texts.full_text: Q: meaning of $GF(2)[x]/(x^3-1)$ What does $GF(2)[x]/(x^3-1)$ mean? I know $GF(2)$ is the Galois field with 2 elements, but what does the forward slash mean? Also, what's the meaning of the entire expression? Thanks! A: $\mbox{GF}(2)$ is the finite field with 2 elements (one of the rare instances in mathematics where the common name for an object is kind of larger than the object itself). $\mbox{GF}(2)[x]$ is the ring of polynomials in the variable $x$ with coefficients in that field. If you're not sure what this means, you should probably learn about this first before you tackle the expression at hand. Let's call this ring $R$. $x^3-1$ is a specific polynomial in that ring. Since the field is $\mbox{GF}(2)$, it's actually the same polynomial as $x^3+1$. $(x^3-1)$ is the ideal generated by that polynomial: it's all the polynomials of the form$x^3-1$, $2(x^3-1)$, $x^3-1+x^2(x^3-1)$, etc., as well as any sums of these. In other words, it's the set of all polynomials that can be written as $p(x)(x^3-1)$ for some polynomial $p(x)$ i

texts.full_text: -n '../' *, but I get lots of false positives. grep is The issue arises because `.` is a special character in regular expressions (it matches any character), so `../` becomes `.\./` in regex syntax, causing false matches like `a/b/c` or `x/y/z`. To fix this, you need to escape the dots to treat them as literal characters or use the `-F` flag to disable regex interpretation. ### Corrected Command: ```bash grep -r -n '\.\./' . ``` ### Explanation: - `-r`: Recursively search directories. - `-n`: Show line numbers.

texts.full_text: Challenge El Cocuy National Park, Colombia The road connecting Tunja and El Cocuy can be easily be described as the worst night bus ride on the planet. Typically, night buses are a great budget way to travel. You can curb the cost of an extra night’s accommodations and get where you want to be with the same ticket. This path, however, only offered only one of those perks. We arrived in Tunja at 8PM and amused ourselves in the terminal until the 10PM departure for El Cocuy. We were the only tourists on the bus, which I had assumed was a direct line. After settling in and getting as comfortable as possible, we slowly drifted off into the desired state of dreamland with omnipresent Colombian music playing throughout the bus (probably to keep the driver awake). I remember waking up to a bump where the pavement stopped abruptly, and the bus began lurching back and forth around zig-zaggy switchbacks as we entered into the mountains. This, along with about 2 dozen stops (every light in the bus gets turned on and passengers start yelling out their stops, which can be anywhere along the road) made the 12-hour bus ride seem like a 24-hour ride. texts.full_text: an ARG?A: Excellent first question! An ARG stands for Alternate Reality Game. It's a type of interactive narrative that uses real-world locations, media channels (like websites and social networks), physical objects like QR codes or RFID tags to create immersive storylines often involving puzzles and clues hidden in the world around us. In essence, an ARG blurs lines between fiction and reality by weaving its stories into our everyday lives. Participants solve mysteries through various forms of digital interaction while also engaging with real-world locations

texts.full_text: Bibliography on English-speaking Quebec by Brendan O’Donnell Toggle navigation Bibliography About Virtual Exhibit Contact FR Le Musée Beaulne au château Morton / The Beaulne Museum in the Morton Residence Type of resource Book Title Le Musée Beaulne au château Morton / The Beaulne Museum in the Morton Residence Abstract The Beaulne Museum, located in the Eastern Townships village of Coaticook, was founded in 1964 by Denise Beaulne. The museum is housed in the former home of Arthur Osmore Norton (1845-1919), a leading Townships industrialist and entrepreneur who played an important role in shaping Coaticook’s economic landscape during his lifetime. The book provides both historical background on Morton's residence, constructed around 1862, as well as details about the life of Arthur Osmore Norton. It also explores how Denise Beaulne transformed this historic building into a museum dedicated to preserving and showcasing artifacts that tell the story of Coaticook’s past. Contents include: - An overview of Morton's construction history - Life in late 19th-century Eastern Townships, focusing on industrial growth and development - Biography of Arthur Osmore Norton: his contributions as an entrepreneur, businessman, and community leader - Denise Beaulne’s vision for the museum - Collection highlights from various periods reflecting local heri
